 {"id":"bd-2kf9","title":"[IDEA-28] Cost/Usage Telemetry + Budget Guardrails","description":"## Background
Docs call out usage metrics and cost estimates, but there is no unified telemetry pipeline or budget enforcement.

## Goals
- Define a standard UsageMetric/CostEstimate object schema.
- Aggregate metrics in host for per-zone budgets.
- Deny or warn when budgets are exceeded (policy-driven).

## Non-goals
- Hard-coded pricing tables; budgets are policy-owned.

## Approach
- Add schema + SDK helpers to emit usage metrics.
- Host aggregates metrics and enforces budget policy.
- CLI exposes budget status for operators.

## Risks
- Inaccurate estimates; keep estimates optional and label confidence.

## Success Criteria
- Over-budget operations are denied with clear reason codes (when configured).
- Metrics are queryable via CLI/API.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Budget enforcement can be configured as warn or deny.
- CLI reports current usage vs budget per zone.

 {"id":"bd-2kzd","title":"[IDEA-04.2] SDK CursorStore implementation","description":"## Goal
Implement `CursorStore` in SDK with lease-aware state writes.

## Details
- Provide APIs: `load_cursor`, `commit_cursor`, `snapshot_cursor`.
- Integrate with lease fencing (singleton_writer).
- Include backoff/retry for state write conflicts.

## Tests
- Unit tests for monotonic progression and conflict resolution.
- Integration test simulating lease loss and failover.

- CursorStore prevents double-polling in multi-node scenarios.

 {"id":"bd-328m","title":"[IDEA-02] RateLimitDeclarations: Manifest + SDK + Host Enforcement","description":"# RateLimitDeclarations: Manifest + SDK + Host Enforcement

## Goal
Make rate limits first-class, declared, and mechanically enforced across SDK, host, and CLI so agents can preflight capacity and connectors behave predictably under throttling.

## Background / Why
The Clawdbot synthesis explicitly calls out rate limit declarations as a cross-cutting need. There is a test bead (`bd-1idx`) but no end-to-end feature work that surfaces declarations in manifests, SDK, host discovery, and CLI outputs.

## Scope
- Extend manifest/operation schema to declare limits (per operation + per pool).
- SDK types + helper to emit runtime `RateLimitStatus`.
- Host discovery exposes limits; CLI surfaces them.

## Deliverables
- Manifest schema update for `rate_limits` on operations and/or connector pools.
- SDK helpers for checking and reporting `RateLimitStatus`.
- Host preflight and discovery include rate limit info.
- CLI `fcp connector info/introspect` prints limits.

## Tests
- Unit tests for schema validation and mapping.
- Integration tests for host discovery responses.
- E2E log coverage with structured log entries for rate limiting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rate limits are visible before invocation.
- Rate limit violations produce deterministic errors + Retry-After hints.
- CLI and JSON outputs include limit metadata.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:28:47.889479096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T16:46:52.583597998Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-328m","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:29:46.047798769Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32k7","title":"TEST-AUDIT: Coverage inventory + mock usage map","description":"# TEST-AUDIT: Coverage inventory + mock usage map

## Goal
Produce a concrete coverage and mock-usage inventory so we can target real-component tests first.

## Deliverables
- Coverage map by crate and module: fcp-core, fcp-protocol, fcp-crypto, fcp-store, fcp-mesh, fcp-sandbox, fcp-host, fcp-cli, fcp-sdk, connectors.
- Mock/fake usage map: enumerate tests that rely on mock_server, wiremock, fake clients, or test doubles.
- Gap list for untested critical paths (protocol parse, crypto verification, revocation freshness, audit/receipt logic, sandbox enforcement).

## Notes
- This task does NOT delete existing mock tests; it produces a replacement plan for no-mock suites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- One report file under docs/testing/ with per-crate coverage estimate and mock usage list.
- Each gap maps to a follow-up bead in this epic.

 {"id":"bd-34lm","title":"CLI: fcp connector (list/info/introspect) - Agent-Visible Discovery","description":"# CLI: `fcp connector` (list/info/introspect) - Agent-Visible Discovery

## Goal
Implement connector discovery commands that agents (and operators) use to find and understand available connectors before invoking tools.

This fills the gap between Agent Integration (`1n78.30`) which defines the discovery API and the CLI which needs to expose it.

## Commands

### `fcp connector list`
List all registered connectors with summary info.

```bash
# Human output
fcp connector list
# ID              NAME          VERSION   TOOLS   RISK    STATUS
# fcp.discord     Discord       1.2.0     12      Risky   ✓ Healthy
# fcp.github      GitHub        2.0.1     24      Risky   ⚠ Degraded (rate limited)
# fcp.slack       Slack         1.0.0     8       Risky   ✓ Healthy
# fcp.anthropic   Anthropic     1.0.0     3       Safe    ✓ Healthy

# Filter by category
fcp connector list --category messaging
fcp connector list --category llm

# Filter by max risk tier
fcp connector list --max-risk safe
fcp connector list --max-risk moderate

# Filter by health
fcp connector list --healthy
fcp connector list --degraded

# JSON output for agents
fcp connector list --json
```

### `fcp connector info <connector>`
Show detailed connector information including manifest metadata.

```bash
fcp connector info fcp.discord
# Connector: fcp.discord
# Name: Discord
# Version: 1.2.0
# Archetype: Bidirectional
# Home Zone: z:private
# 
# Capabilities Required:
#   - discord.messages.read (Safe)
#   - discord.messages.send (Risky)
#   - discord.webhooks.manage (Dangerous)
# 
# Network Constraints:
#   Allowed: discord.com:443, gateway.discord.gg:443
#   CIDR Deny: default (RFC1918 + localhost)
# 
# State Model: singleton_writer
# Event Topics: message.new, message.edited, member.joined
# 
# Health: Healthy
# Uptime: 3d 14h 22m
# Last Health Check: 2s ago

fcp connector info fcp.discord --json
```

### `fcp connector introspect <connector>`
Return MCP-compatible tool descriptors for a connector.

```bash
# Human-readable tool list
fcp connector introspect fcp.discord
# Tools (12):
#   send_message      Send a message to a channel           Risky
#   send_dm           Send a direct message                 Risky  
#   list_channels     List channels in a guild              Safe
#   get_message       Get a specific message                Safe
#   ...

# Full MCP tool descriptors (JSON)
fcp connector introspect fcp.discord --json

# Single tool
fcp connector introspect fcp.discord --tool send_message --json
```

## Output Contracts

### ConnectorSummary (list)
```rust
pub struct ConnectorSummary {
    pub id: ConnectorId,
    pub name: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub version: semver::Version,
    pub categories: Vec<String>,
    pub tool_count: u32,
    pub max_risk_tier: SafetyTier,
    pub enabled: bool,
    pub health: ConnectorHealth,
}
```

### ConnectorInfo (info)
```rust
pub struct ConnectorInfo {
    pub summary: ConnectorSummary,
    pub archetype: ConnectorArchetype,
    pub home_zone: ZoneId,
    pub capabilities: CapabilityDeclarations,
    pub network_constraints: NetworkConstraints,
    pub state_model: ConnectorStateModel,
    pub event_topics: Vec<EventTopic>,
    pub manifest_hash: ObjectId,
    pub installed_at: DateTime<Utc>,
}
```

### ToolDescriptor (introspect) - SEP-1382 compliant
```rust
pub struct ToolDescriptor {
    pub name: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub input_schema: serde_json::Value,
    pub output_schema: serde_json::Value,
    pub risk_tier: SafetyTier,
    pub requires_confirmation: bool,
    pub idempotent: bool,
    pub supports_simulate: bool,
    pub latency_hint: LatencyHint,
    pub rate_limits: Option<Vec<String>>,
    pub examples: Option<Vec<ToolExample>>,
    pub ai_hints: Option<String>,
}
```

## Tests

### Unit Tests
- test_connector_list_filters_by_category
- test_connector_list_filters_by_risk
- test_connector_list_filters_by_health
- test_connector_info_includes_all_manifest_data
- test_connector_introspect_returns_mcp_compliant_descriptors
- test_connector_introspect_single_tool

### Integration Tests
- test_connector_list_matches_registry
- test_connector_introspect_schema_valid

### Snapshot Tests
- connector_list_human.snap
- connector_list_json.snap
- connector_info_human.snap
- connector_info_json.snap
- connector_introspect_human.snap
- connector_introspect_json.snap

### E2E Script
e2e/connector_discovery.sh with detailed logging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `fcp connector list` returns all registered connectors
- [ ] Filtering by category, risk, and health works correctly
- [ ] `fcp connector info` returns full manifest-derived data
- [ ] `fcp connector introspect` returns MCP-compliant tool descriptors
- [ ] All commands support `--json` output
- [ ] No secrets are included in any output
- [ ] Commands work in offline mode with cached data","notes":"Claimed by OlivePuma to implement CLI list/info/introspect filters + align output with host discovery types.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OlivePuma","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:22:31.832137848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:14:20.377269627Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent","cli","fcp2","mvp"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-34lm","depends_on_id":"bd-2h7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:17:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-34lm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:17:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-34lm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:17:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-34lm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-oip0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:17:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-355n","title":"[IDEA-16.1] Requirements index parser + validation CLI","description":"## What
Implement a CLI that loads `docs/STANDARD_Requirements_Index.md` and validates:
- referenced bead IDs exist
- required owner/test fields are present
- optional notes fields are well-formed

## How
- Parse markdown tables into structured rows.
- Load beads from `br list --json` (or `.beads/issues.jsonl`) and build a lookup.
- Emit JSON + human summary; non-zero exit on errors.

## Risks
- False positives on formatting changes → parser must ignore whitespace and tolerate column shifts.

## Success Criteria
- CLI can be run locally and in CI with consistent output.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Parses all tables without panics on extra whitespace or column reordering.
- Detects duplicate rows and missing Owner/Test entries.
- Emits JSON report with `errors[]` and `warnings[]` arrays.
